data.text: Let me read the relevant files to understand the context before writing the tests.

data.output:
300	
301	### TC-015: Read Federation
302	
303	Verifies FEAT-026 hub/spoke pull aggregation.
304	
305	| ID | Test | Acceptance | Status |
306	|----|------|------------|--------|
307	| TC-015.1 | Hub with no spokes | `/federation` renders hub status and an empty spoke list | Planned |
308	| TC-015.2 | Spoke registration | A spoke registers and heartbeats to the hub with version/capability metadata | Planned |
309	| TC-015.3 | Federated reads | Hub combined bead/run/project views merge local and spoke rows with node/project badges | Planned |
310	| TC-015.4 | Stale/offline/degraded | Slow, unreachable, stale, and version-skewed spokes produce partial results and visible status badges | Planned |
311	
312	### TC-016: Worker Single Pane and Server-Managed Work
313	
314	Verifies FEAT-021 US-100/US-101. This is the basic "server runs work and the
315	operator sees it" contract. The fixture uses local JSONL-backed projects and
316	existing autonomous workers; Axon and 2k-scale performance are out of scope.
317	
318	| ID | Test | Acceptance | Status |
319	|----|------|------------|--------|
320	| TC-016.1 | Node-wide workers | `/nodes/:nodeId/workers` lists workers from at least two registered projects with project badge, worker id, state, current bead id/title, phase, route/model, elapsed time, and freshness | Planned |
321	| TC-016.2 | Worker detail | Clicking a worker opens detail with recent phase events, captured output, prompt, attempt id, current bead link, and stop action | Planned |
322	| TC-016.3 | Start multiple workers | Project workers page starts `count=2` autonomous `ddx work` workers through `startWorker`; both rows appear and the queue/worker summary updates | Planned |
323	| TC-016.4 | Worker cap | Starting beyond `.ddx/config.yaml workers.max_count` is refused or disabled with an operator-visible cap reason | Planned |
324	| TC-016.5 | Stop worker | Stop action reaches a stopping/terminal state and records a lifecycle/audit event | Planned |
325	| TC-016.6 | Live updates | Worker row updates current bead and phase without page reload via subscription or polling fallback | Planned |
326	
327	### TC-017: Federated Worker Control and Owner-Targeted Writes
328	
329	Verifies FEAT-026 US-097b and FEAT-021 US-102. The hub is a single pane of glass,
330	but the owning node executes every mutation locally.
331	
332	| ID | Test | Acceptance | Status |
333	|----|------|------------|--------|
334	| TC-017.1 | Federated worker list | Hub `/nodes/:hubId/workers?scope=federation` lists hub and spoke workers with node/project badges and freshness | Planned |
335	| TC-017.2 | Start spoke worker | Starting a worker for a spoke project from the hub forwards to exactly that spoke; the worker is created on the spoke, not the hub | Planned |
336	| TC-017.3 | Stop spoke worker | Stopping a spoke worker from the hub forwards to the owning spoke and the hub view reflects the terminal state | Planned |
337	| TC-017.4 | Offline refusal | Commands for an offline spoke are refused with an operator-visible reason and do not create phantom local rows | Planned |
338	| TC-017.5 | Idempotency | Retrying a forwarded command with the same request id returns the original result without launching duplicates or appending duplicate events | Planned |
339	
340	### TC-018: Cross-Project Bead Queue Mutations
341	
342	Verifies FEAT-021 US-103. Queue stewardship must be possible from the web UI for
343	any registered project.
344	
345	| ID | Test | Acceptance | Status |
346	|----|------|------------|--------|
347	| TC-018.1 | Create bead | Creating a bead with title, description, acceptance, priority, type, and labels persists it in the selected project only | Planned |
348	| TC-018.2 | Edit bead | Editing title, labels, priority, description, acceptance, or status persists and updates the row without cross-project leakage | Planned |
349	| TC-018.3 | Lifecycle actions | Approve, block, cancel, and reopen mutate lifecycle state, append events, and update queue summary | Planned |
350	| TC-018.4 | Federated bead write | Hub create/edit for a spoke project forwards to the owning spoke and appears in federated reads with origin/forwarding audit metadata | Planned |
351	| TC-018.5 | Invalid state refusal | Empty required reason/note, stale state, or non-owned target is rejected with a visible error | Planned |
352	
353	### TC-019: Cross-Project Spec Editing
354	
355	Verifies FEAT-021 US-104. Spec editing is scoped to project documents,
356	especially `docs/helix/**`.
357	
358	| ID | Test | Acceptance | Status |
359	|----|------|------------|--------|

data.text: Now let me check the feature spec and existing fixtures:

data.output:
480	- Given a run produced an artifact, then the detail route links to the artifact
481	  browser entry and that artifact links back to the producing run
482	
483	**E2E Test:** `runs.spec.ts` — full workflow: navigate to project runs → apply layer filter → verify URL → click work record → verify work detail fields → click child try → verify try fields → click layer-1 run → verify all fields → follow evidence link → press Back through breadcrumbs to run list → verify filter state restored → follow artifact link from run → verify artifact links back to run
484	
485	### US-100: Operator Sees Every Worker in One Pane
486	**As an** operator running DDx workers across projects and nodes
487	**I want** one Workers workbench that shows every worker with node and project
488	context
489	**So that** I can understand what is running without opening shells on each
490	machine
491	
492	**Acceptance Criteria:**
493	- Given two projects on one node each have active or recently-reported workers,
494	  when I open `/nodes/:nodeId/workers`, then I see all workers with project
495	  badges, worker state, current bead id/title, phase, model/route, elapsed time,
496	  and last-event freshness.
497	- Given the node is a federation hub with one spoke, when I set
498	  `scope=federation`, then I see hub and spoke workers in the same table with
499	  node badges and direct fallback URLs for spoke rows.
500	- Given a worker stops reporting, then its row transitions from `connected` to
501	  `stale` or `disconnected` without disappearing.
502	- Given I click a worker row, then a detail view shows recent phase events,
503	  captured output, prompt, attempt id, current bead link, and stop action.
504	
505	**E2E Test:** `workers-single-pane.spec.ts` — full workflow: seed two projects
506	with reported workers, open node workers, verify badges and current bead fields,
507	open detail, verify prompt/output/events, then run a two-node fixture and verify
508	`scope=federation` merges hub and spoke workers with freshness badges.
509	
510	### US-101: Operator Starts Server-Managed Work Capacity
511	**As an** operator watching a ready queue
512	**I want** to start more `ddx work` workers from the server UI
513	**So that** the server handles worker launch, tracking, and stop control
514	
515	**Acceptance Criteria:**
516	- Given a project has ready beads, when I click `+ Add worker` and request
517	  count `2`, mode `watch`, and an optional label filter, then the server starts
518	  two autonomous workers for that project and the Workers table shows both.
519	- Given the project has `workers.max_count` configured, then the UI disables or
520	  rejects starts that would exceed the cap and shows the cap reason.
521	- Given a newly-started worker claims a bead, then the worker row updates with
522	  current bead id/title and phase without a page reload.
523	- Given I click Stop on a running worker, then the server requests stop and the
524	  row reaches a terminal or stopping state with an audit event.
525	
526	**E2E Test:** `workers-dispatch.spec.ts` — full workflow: open project workers,
527	start two workers, verify rows and queue summary, verify cap refusal, stop one
528	worker, and verify terminal state plus audit event.
529

1	# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
2	# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
3	# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook
4	
5	# Configuration
6	colors: true
7	no_tty: false
8	assertion: false
9	
10	# Reusable command groups
11	command_groups:
12	  go_checks: &go_checks
13	    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
14	    tags: [go, backend]
15	
16	  security_checks: &security_checks
17	    tags: [security]
18	
19	  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
20	    tags: [format, style]
21	
22	# ====================
23	# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
24	# ====================
25	pre-commit:
26	  parallel: true
27	
28	  commands:
29	    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
30	    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
31	    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
32	    conflicts:
33	      name: "Check merge conflicts"
34	      run: |
35	        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
36	        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
37	        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
38	          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
39	          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
40	        done | head -1)
41	        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
42	          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
43	          exit 1
44	        fi
45	        exit 0
46	      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."
47	
48	    git-config-health:
49	      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
50	      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before committing."
51	
52	    beads-tracker-health:
53	      run: "sh scripts/beads-tracker-health.sh"
54	      fail_text: "Beads tracker has unstaged changes. Run 'git add .ddx/beads.jsonl'."
55	
56	    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
57	    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
58	    # debug-go:
59	    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
60	    #   glob: "*.go"
61	    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
62	    #   run: |
63	    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
64	    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
65	    #     else
66	    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
67	    #     fi
68	    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"
69	
70	    debug-python:
71	      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
72	      glob: "*.py"
73	      run: |
74	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
75	          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
76	        else
77	          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
78	        fi
79	      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"
80	
81	    # Go formatting - native gofmt
82	    go-fmt:
83	      <<: *go_checks
84	      name: "Go Format"
85	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
86	      run: |
87	        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
88	        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
89	          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
90	          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
91	          exit 1
92	        fi
93	      skip:
94	        - merge
95	        - rebase
96	
97	    # Go linting - golangci-lint required
98	    go-lint:
99	      <<: *go_checks
100	      name: "Go Lint"
101	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
102	      run: |
103	        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
104	          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
105	          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
106	          exit 1
107	        fi
108	        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.
109	        # Skip Go testdata directories — these contain analyzer fixtures
110	        # that intentionally reference stub packages and only typecheck
111	        # under analysistest's GOPATH-style loader.
112	        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '/testdata/' || true)
113	        [ -z "$packages" ] && exit 0
114	        for pkg in $packages; do
115	          # Unset GIT_DIR so revgrep (used by --new-from-rev) computes the diff
116	          # against the actual work tree, not an empty one. Git sets GIT_DIR in
117	          # hook context pointing at the worktree's .git subdir; when a subprocess
118	          # runs git diff with only GIT_DIR set (no GIT_WORK_TREE), the work tree
119	          # appears empty and revgrep panics on function-name contexts in hunks.
120	          env -u GIT_DIR -u GIT_INDEX_FILE golangci-lint run --timeout=60s --new-from-rev=HEAD~1 "$pkg"
121	        done
122	      skip:
123	        - merge
124	        - rebase
125	
126	    # Go build - only for changed packages
127	    go-build:
128	      <<: *go_checks
129	      name: "Go Build"
130	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
131	      run: 'go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...'
132	      skip:
133	        - merge
134	        - rebase
135	
136	    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage for the
137	    # no-unbounded-prompts invariant. Scoped to packages containing
138	    # staged files, with diagnostics filtered to only those staged
139	    # files so existing violations in untouched packages don't block
140	    # unrelated commits. Stages B–E migrate the remaining historical
141	    # sites; full-tree enforcement lives in the `ci` block below.
142	    evidence-lint:
143	      <<: *go_checks
144	      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts)"
145	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
146	      run: |
147	        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
148	        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
149	        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/evidencelint' || true)
150	        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
151	        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
152	        violations=""
153	        for f in $staged; do
154	          bare="${f#cli/}"
155	          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
156	          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
157	            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
158	          fi
159	        done
160	        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
161	          printf '%s' "$violations"
162	          exit 1
163	        fi
164	        exit 0
165	      skip:
166	        - merge
167	        - rebase
168	      fail_text: "Unbounded prompt sink detected in staged file. Route through internal/evidence or add `// evidence:allow-unbounded reason=\"...\"`."
169	
170	    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: forbidden durable-knob fields on
171	    # *Runtime structs and reintroduction of legacy *Options types.
172	    # Scoped to packages containing staged files, with diagnostics
173	    # filtered to only those staged files so existing violations in
174	    # untouched packages don't block unrelated commits. The full-tree
175	    # gate in the `ci` block fires once the RunOptions/CompareOptions/
176	    # QuorumOptions retirement beads land.
177	    runtime-lint:
178	      <<: *go_checks
179	      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options)"
180	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
181	      run: |
182	        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
183	        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
184	        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/runtimelint' || true)
185	        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
186	        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
187	        violations=""
188	        for f in $staged; do
189	          bare="${f#cli/}"
190	          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
191	          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
192	            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
193	          fi
194	        done
195	        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
196	          printf '%s' "$violations"
197	          exit 1
198	        fi
199	        exit 0
200	      skip:
201	        - merge
202	        - rebase
203	      fail_text: "Forbidden runtime-struct field or legacy *Options type in staged file. Move durable knobs to config.ResolvedConfig and use the *Runtime pair (SD-024 §Stage 4 / TD-024 §Lint rule)."
204	
205	    # Go tests - targeted testing
206	    go-test:
207	      <<: *go_checks
208	      name: "Go Tests"
209	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
210	      run: |
211	        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
212	        for pkg in $packages; do
213	          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
214	            go test -short -race -timeout 30m "./$pkg"
215	          fi
216	        done
217	      skip:
218	        - merge
219	        - rebase
220	      env:
221	        CGO_ENABLED: "1"
222	        SKIP_INTEGRATION_TESTS: "true"
223	
224	    # Secrets scanning - prefer gitleaks when available
225	    secrets:
226	      <<: *security_checks
227	      name: "Scan for secrets"
228	      run: |
229	        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
230	          gitleaks protect --staged --verbose --redact
231	        else
232	          # Fallback to staged-added-line pattern matching when gitleaks is absent.
233	          # Scanning whole tracker JSONL files causes noisy false positives on prose.
234	          added_lines=$(git diff --cached --unified=0 --no-color -- {staged_files} | grep '^+' | grep -v '^+++')
235	
236	          patterns='((api[_-]?key|apikey|secret|password|passwd|pwd|credential|private[_-]?key|access[_-]?token|auth[_-]?token)[[:space:]]*[:=][[:space:]]*["'"'"'][A-Za-z0-9_./+=-]{20,})|(Authorization:[[:space:]]*Bearer[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z0-9._-]{20,})'
237	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E "$patterns"; then
238	            echo "Potential secrets detected"
239	            exit 1
240	          fi
241	
242	          # AWS keys
243	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}'; then
244	            echo "AWS key pattern detected"
245	            exit 1
246	          fi
247	
248	          # GitHub tokens
249	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}'; then
250	            echo "GitHub token pattern detected"
251	            exit 1
252	          fi
253	        fi
254	      exclude:
255	        - "*.md"
256	        - "go.sum"
257	        - "package-lock.json"
258	        - "*_test.go"
259	        - "**/dist/**"
260	        - "scripts/test-secrets-fallback.sh"
261	      fail_text: "Secrets detected! Remove them or add to .gitleaks.toml if false positive"
262	
263	    # Binary and large file check
264	    large-files:
265	      name: "Check file sizes"
266	      run: |
267	        for file in {staged_files}; do
268	          size=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
269	          if [ "$size" -gt 5242880 ]; then
270	            echo "Large file detected: $file ($(($size / 1048576))MB)"
271	            exit 1
272	          fi
273	        done
274	      exclude:
275	        - "*.png"
276	        - "*.jpg"
277	        - "*.jpeg"
278	        - "*.gif"
279	        - "*.svg"
280	        - "*.ico"
281	        - "*.pdf"
282	        - "*.woff"
283	        - "*.woff2"
284	        - "*.ttf"
285	        - "*.eot"
286	        # bead store grows monotonically with queue activity; expected to exceed 5MB
287	        - ".ddx/beads.jsonl"
288	        # archive partner accumulates closed beads forever; trends past 5MB
289	        - ".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl"
290	      fail_text: "Large files detected. Use Git LFS for files over 5MB"
291	
292	    # DDx validation - only when relevant files change
293	    ddx-validate:
294	      name: "Validate DDx"
295	      run: |
296	        # Only run if DDx files are staged
297	        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '(\.ddx\.yml|templates/|patterns/|prompts/)'; then
298	          exit 0
299	        fi
300	
301	        # Strip git's local-env-vars before invoking ddx so any git commands
302	        # the ddx process spawns honor cmd.Dir / -C <dir> rather than the
303	        # GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / etc. inherited from this hook's context.
304	        # Without this, ddx's git invocations mutate THIS repo (the hook's),
305	        # producing core.bare=true and stray core.worktree corruption.
306	        # ddx-64ac553a Phase 0 (defensive triage; real fix is the central
307	        # git.Command wrapper in Phase 2a).
308	        unset $(git rev-parse --local-env-vars)
309	
310	        # Collect staged paths and pass them to --paths so doctor only validates
311	        # the checks relevant to the staged files (config or resource), skipping
312	        # global installation checks (e.g. legacy skill symlinks) that are
313	        # unrelated to the content of the staged change. [ddx-777f4a09]
314	        staged_paths="$(git diff --cached --name-only | tr '\n' ' ')"
315	
316	        # Check for DDx command availability
317	        if command -v ddx >/dev/null 2>&1; then
318	          ddx doctor --paths "$staged_paths" >/dev/null 2>&1
319	        elif [ -x "./cli/ddx" ]; then
320	          ./cli/ddx doctor --paths "$staged_paths" >/dev/null 2>&1
321	        fi
322	      fail_text: "DDx validation failed. Run 'ddx doctor' for details"
323	
324	    skill-schema:
325	      <<: *go_checks
326	      name: "Validate skill metadata"
327	      run: |
328	        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^(library/skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md|cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md)$'; then
329	          exit 0
330	        fi
331	        make skill-schema
332	      skip:
333	        - merge
334	        - rebase
335	
336	    sync-embedded-default-plugin:
337	      name: "Sync cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library from library/"
338	      root: "cli/"
339	      run: |
340	        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^library/(skills/|package\.yaml$)'; then
341	          exit 0
342	        fi
343	        make copy-skills
344	        cd .. && git add cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/
345	      stage_fixed: true
346	      skip:
347	        - merge
348	        - rebase
349	      fail_text: "Failed to sync cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library from library/. Run 'cd cli && make copy-skills' manually and stage the result."
350	
351	    design-md-lint:
352	      name: "DESIGN.md lint"
353	      glob: ".stitch/DESIGN.md"
354	      run: |
355	        if ! command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1; then
356	          echo "bun not found; skipping DESIGN.md lint"
357	          exit 0
358	        fi
359	        bun install --frozen-lockfile 2>/dev/null || bun install
360	        bunx @google/design.md lint .stitch/DESIGN.md
361	      fail_text: "DESIGN.md lint failed. Run: bunx @google/design.md lint .stitch/DESIGN.md"
362	
363	    test-engineer-persona-drift:
364	      name: "Guard test-engineer persona (ddx-482fad2f)"
365	      glob: "library/personas/test-engineer.md"
366	      run: |
367	        if grep -q "Mock all external dependencies" library/personas/test-engineer.md; then
368	          echo "ERROR: 'Mock all external dependencies' is forbidden in the test-engineer persona (see ddx-482fad2f)"
369	          exit 1
370	        fi
371	        if grep -q "test doubles (mocks" library/personas/test-engineer.md; then
372	          echo "ERROR: 'test doubles (mocks, stubs, spies)' lists mocks and stubs as equivalent — forbidden (see ddx-482fad2f)"
373	          exit 1
374	        fi
375	      fail_text: "test-engineer persona contains forbidden mock-promotion phrases"
376	
377	    no-tmp-in-test-factory:
378	      name: "Guard NewCommandFactory(\"/tmp\") in tests (ddx-9063101c)"
379	      glob: "cli/cmd/*_test.go"
380	      run: |
381	        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/cmd/[^/]+_test\.go$' || true)
382	        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
383	        bad=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs grep -lF 'NewCommandFactory("/tmp")' 2>/dev/null || true)
384	        if [ -n "$bad" ]; then
385	          printf '%s\n' "$bad" | xargs grep -nF 'NewCommandFactory("/tmp")'
386	          exit 1
387	        fi
388	        exit 0
389	      fail_text: "NewCommandFactory(\"/tmp\") found in cli/cmd/*_test.go — use t.TempDir() instead"
390	
391	    ddxroot-path-audit:
392	      name: "Guard hardcoded DDx state-root callsites"
393	      run: |
394	        if rg -n 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"|filepath\.Join\(\s*(projectRoot|[A-Za-z0-9_\.]*WorkingDir)\s*,\s*[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*RelPath\s*\)|(projectRoot|[A-Za-z0-9_\.]*WorkingDir)\s*\+\s*"/\.ddx(?:/|")' cli/ --type go | grep -Ev '(^cli/internal/ddxroot/)|(_test\.go$)'; then
395	          echo "Hardcoded DDx state-root callsites found outside cli/internal/ddxroot/ and approved test fixtures"
396	          exit 1
397	        fi
398	        exit 0
399	      fail_text: "Hardcoded DDx state-root callsites found outside cli/internal/ddxroot/. Route project state paths through internal/ddxroot."
400	
401	    skill-dir-anchor:
402	      name: "Skill dirs must have SKILL.md"
403	      run: |
404	        failed=0
405	        for base in .agents/skills .claude/skills; do
406	          [ -d "$base" ] || continue
407	          for d in $(ls -A "$base"); do
408	            full="$base/$d"
409	            [ -d "$full" ] || continue
410	            if [ ! -f "$full/SKILL.md" ]; then
411	              echo "ERROR: skill-dir $full has no SKILL.md; skill directories must be real skills with a SKILL.md anchor. If this is contamination, remove the directory. If this is a new skill, add a SKILL.md."
412	              failed=1
413	            fi
414	          done
415	        done
416	        exit $failed
417	      fail_text: "Skill directory missing SKILL.md. Either add SKILL.md (new skill) or remove the directory (contamination)."
418	
419	# ====================
420	# CI/CD - Full validation (all files)
421	# ====================
422	ci:
423	  parallel: true
424	
425	  commands:
426	    # Full conflict check
427	    conflicts-all:
428	      name: "Check all merge conflicts"
429	      run: |
430	        # Check for actual merge conflict markers (exactly 7 characters followed by space or newline)
431	        conflicts=$(find . -type f ! -path "./.git/*" ! -path "./cli/.git/*" ! -name "lefthook.yml" \
432	          ! -path "*/node_modules/*" ! -path "*/.svelte-kit/*" ! -path "./cli/internal/server/frontend/build/*" \
433	          ! -path "./website/public/*" ! -path "./cli/build/*" \
434	          -exec grep -l "^<<<<<<<[ \t]\|^=======\$\|^>>>>>>>[ \t]\|^||||||[ \t]" {} \; 2>/dev/null | head -1)
435	        if [ -n "$conflicts" ]; then
436	          echo "Merge conflict markers found in: $conflicts"
437	          exit 1
438	        fi
439	
440	    # Full secrets scan
441	    secrets-all:
442	      <<: *security_checks
443	      name: "Full secrets scan"
444	      run: |
445	        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
446	          gitleaks detect --verbose --redact
447	        else
448	          echo "Consider installing gitleaks for comprehensive secret scanning"
449	        fi
450	
451	    # Go full test suite
452	    go-test-all:
453	      <<: *go_checks
454	      name: "Full Go test suite"
455	      root: "cli/"
456	      run: 'go test -race -cover ./...'
457	
458	    bead-schema:
459	      <<: *go_checks
460	      name: "Bead Schema Guard"
461	      run: 'make bead-schema'
462	
463	    skill-schema:
464	      <<: *go_checks
465	      name: "Skill Metadata Guard"
466	      run: 'make skill-schema'
467	
468	    # Go formatting check all files
469	    go-fmt-all:
470	      <<: *go_checks
471	      name: "Check all Go formatting"
472	      root: "cli/"
473	      run: |
474	        unformatted=$(gofmt -l .)
475	        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
476	          echo "Unformatted files: $unformatted"
477	          exit 1
478	        fi
479	
480	    # Go lint all files
481	    go-lint-all:
482	      <<: *go_checks
483	      name: "Lint all Go files"
484	      root: "cli/"
485	      run: |
486	        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
487	          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
488	          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
489	          exit 1
490	        fi
491	        golangci-lint run --timeout=5m
492	
493	    # Go build all targets
494	    go-build-all:
495	      <<: *go_checks
496	      name: "Build all targets"
497	      root: "cli/"
498	      run: 'go build -v ./...'
499	
500	    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage. Repo-wide
501	    # invariant check; runs in CI even when no Go files were staged
502	    # in the triggering commit so historical regressions still surface.
503	    evidence-lint-all:
504	      <<: *go_checks
505	      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts) — full tree"
506	      root: "cli/"
507	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint ./...'
508	
509	    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: full-tree gate. The pre-commit hook
510	    # only checks staged-package diagnostics; this CI gate enforces the
511	    # post-cleanup invariant — zero forbidden runtime fields and zero
512	    # legacy *Options types anywhere in cli/. Activated now that the
513	    # RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions/ExecuteBeadOptions
514	    # retirement beads have all landed (ddx-ade5ebb3, ddx-a8eaef0a).
515	    runtime-lint-all:
516	      <<: *go_checks
517	      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options) — full tree"
518	      root: "cli/"
519	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...'
520	
521	    ddxroot-path-audit-all:
522	      name: "Guard hardcoded DDx state-root callsites — full tree"
523	      run: |
524	        if rg -n 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"|filepath\.Join\(\s*(projectRoot|[A-Za-z0-9_\.]*WorkingDir)\s*,\s*[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*RelPath\s*\)|(projectRoot|[A-Za-z0-9_\.]*WorkingDir)\s*\+\s*"/\.ddx(?:/|")' cli/ --type go | grep -Ev '(^cli/internal/ddxroot/)|(_test\.go$)'; then
525	          echo "Hardcoded DDx state-root callsites found outside cli/internal/ddxroot/ and approved test fixtures"
526	          exit 1
527	        fi
528	        exit 0
529	
530	    # FEAT-006 routing-lint: dead-code / anti-reintroduction gate for
531	    # the compensating DDx-side routing helpers retired by ddx-3bd7396a
532	    # (parent epic ddx-fdd3ea36 §AC#7, cleanup bead ddx-653f6ac9). Fails
533	    # CI if any of ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef,
534	    # AdaptiveMinTier, workersByHarness, or the retired --escalate /
535	    # --override-model / agent.routing.profile_ladders /
536	    # agent.routing.model_overrides flag/config tokens reappear in
537	    # cli/ Go source.
538	    routing-lint-all:
539	      <<: *go_checks
540	      name: "Routing Lint (FEAT-006 compensating-routing helpers retired) — full tree"
541	      root: "cli/"
542	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./...'
543	
544	    docs-routing-lint:
545	      name: "Docs Routing Lint (legacy DDx routing surfaces allowlisted)"
546	      run: 'bash scripts/check-legacy-routing-docs.sh'
547	
548	    # YAML validation
549	    yaml-validate:
550	      name: "Validate YAML files"
551	      glob: "**/*.{yml,yaml}"
552	      run: |
553	        if command -v yq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
554	          for file in {all_files}; do
555	            yq eval . "$file" >/dev/null || exit 1
556	          done
557	        fi
558	
559	# ====================
560	# Pre-push hooks - Extended validation
561	# ====================
562	pre-push:
563	  parallel: false
564	
565	  commands:
566	    git-config-health:
567	      name: "Git config health"
568	      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
569	      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before pushing."
570	      tags: [policy]
571	
572	    # AGENTS.md §Merge Policy: branches carrying execute-bead / [ddx-*]
573	    # commits must preserve SHA history. closing_commit_sha pointers in
574	    # .ddx/beads.jsonl index those exact commits; squash/rebase/amend that
575	    # rewrites them destroys the output = bead(input) guarantee.
576	    # This guard rejects a push if the local branch contains execute-bead
577	    # or [ddx-*] tagged commits AND the remote already has a different
578	    # history for them (i.e. a force-push rewrite is in flight).
579	    merge-policy:
580	      name: "Preserve execute-bead history"
581	      run: |
582	        # {1} = remote name (origin); {2} = remote URL
583	        remote="${1:-origin}"
584	        # Gather the commits being pushed. lefthook sets {push_files}
585	        # for file pushes; for commit-level inspection we use git range.
586	        # Find the remote-tracking branch HEAD to compute what's new.
587	        local_ref="$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
588	        [ -z "$local_ref" ] && exit 0
589	        remote_ref="refs/remotes/$remote/${local_ref#refs/heads/}"
590	        if ! git rev-parse --verify "$remote_ref" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
591	          # No remote tracking yet — first push, nothing to rewrite.
592	          exit 0
593	        fi
594	        # Commits local has that remote does not (being pushed).
595	        new_commits="$(git log --format='%H %s' "$remote_ref"..HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
596	        [ -z "$new_commits" ] && exit 0
597	        # Commits remote has that local dropped (rewritten or pruned).
598	        dropped="$(git log --format='%H %s' HEAD.."$remote_ref" 2>/dev/null)"
599	        [ -z "$dropped" ] && exit 0
600	        # Check for execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers in dropped commits.
601	        pattern='execute-bead|\[ddx-[0-9a-f]{8,}\]'
602	        if printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -qE "$pattern"; then
603	          echo "ERROR: push would drop commits with execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers:"
604	          printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -E "$pattern" | head
605	          echo ""
606	          echo "AGENTS.md §Merge Policy forbids rewriting these SHAs because"
607	          echo ".ddx/beads.jsonl closing_commit_sha points at them."
608	          echo "Use --ff-only or --no-ff; do not squash, rebase, or amend."
609	          exit 1
610	        fi
611	        exit 0
612	      tags: [policy]
613	
614	    # Ensure no debug statements in any file
615	    no-debug:
616	      name: "No debug statements"
617	      run: |
618	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
619	          rg "(console\.log|console\.debug\(|console\.error\(\"DEBUG|fmt\.Print(ln|f)?\(\"DEBUG|pdb\.set_trace|debugger|breakpoint\(\))" \
620	            --type-add 'code:*.{go,js,jsx,ts,tsx,py}' -t code
621	          status=$?
622	          # rg exit codes: 0=match found (fail), 1=no match (pass), 2+=error (fail closed)
623	          if [ "$status" -eq 1 ]; then
624	            exit 0
625	          fi
626	          exit 1
627	        fi
628	
629	# Skip configuration
630	# Usage: LEFTHOOK_EXCLUDE=go-test,secrets git commit
631	# Or: LEFTHOOK=0 git commit (skip all hooks)
632	
633	# Install hooks: lefthook install
634	# Run manually: lefthook run pre-commit
635	# Run CI checks: lefthook run ci
636

input.description: Commit the two new E2E test files

data.text:
All checks passed. The commit landed cleanly at `cc026d631`.

**Summary:** Added two new Playwright E2E spec files:

- `e2e/workers-single-pane.spec.ts` — 3 tests covering TC-016.1/2 and the federation scope row (US-100): `shows all local project workers in one node-wide pane`, `opens worker detail with current bead and captured output`, `shows hub and spoke workers in federation scope`

- `e2e/workers-dispatch.spec.ts` — 3 tests covering TC-016.3/4/5 (US-101): `starts two server managed work workers`, `refuses starts beyond workers max count`, `stops a worker and shows the lifecycle event`

Both files use static fixture identifiers and mocked GraphQL routes — no live developer DDx state required. Go tests (`./internal/server/...`) and `lefthook run pre-commit` both pass.
